steps.title: What the task requires
steps.content: The student must explain an advantage of the Spruce Tree House site — that means naming a specific benefit AND connecting it to a detail from the notes that supports it. Simply mentioning the site or its location is not enough.

steps.title: Eliminate three, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Since it was built under a rock overhang, Spruce Tree House was naturally protected from heavy rain and snow.': Names the advantage (weather protection) and anchors it to the specific site feature (rock overhang) from the notes. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The location of Spruce Tree House...provided an advantage to its inhabitants.': States an advantage exists but does not explain what the advantage is. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Located in southwestern Colorado, Spruce Tree House is an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site that was inhabited from approximately 1200-1280 CE.': Only reports location and date — no advantage mentioned at all. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The relatively flat terrain on which Spruce Tree House was built allowed for the construction of large terraced buildings.': Applies Ansel Hall's details to Spruce Tree House, which had a rock overhang, not flat terrain. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
